_DOCUMENTATION.model: SIMPLE Crop Model — Zhao C. et al. (2019). Eur. J. Agron. 104, 97-107.
_DOCUMENTATION.parameter_source: Table 1a of project document (pages 17-18). ALL numeric crop values below are taken directly from that table.

units.t_sum: °C·d — total thermal time from sowing to physiological maturity
units.hi: dimensionless (0-1) — harvest index = harvestable yield / total biomass
units.i_50a: °C·d — cultivar FIXED: cumulative TT at which canopy intercepts 50% radiation during leaf growth
units.i_50b: °C·d — cultivar STARTING value: cumulative TT for 50% radiation loss during senescence; increases daily with heat/water stress
units.i_50maxH: °C·d per day — species: maximum daily increase in I50B due to heat stress (accelerates senescence)
units.i_50maxW: °C·d per day — species: maximum daily increase in I50B due to water stress (accelerates senescence)
units.t_base: °C — base temperature (GDD = 0 below this, fTemp = 0 below this)
units.t_opt: °C — optimal temperature (fTemp = 1.0 at and above this)
units.t_heat_onset: °C — daily Tmax above which heat stress factor (fHeat) begins to drop below 1
units.t_extreme: °C — daily Tmax at which fHeat = 0 (growth/biomass completely halted)
units.rue: g MJ-1 — radiation use efficiency at reference CO2=350ppm, no stress
units.f_solar_max: dimensionless (0-1) — maximum canopy solar radiation interception fraction
units.s_co2: per ppm — CO2 sensitivity (fCO2 = 1 + s_co2 × (CO2 - 350)); table value divided by 100
units.s_water: dimensionless — water stress sensitivity coefficient (fWater = max(0, 1 - s_water × ARID))
units.co2_reference: ppm — reference CO2 concentration where fCO2 = 1.0
units.co2_saturation: ppm — CO2 at which fCO2 saturates: fCO2 = 1 + s_co2 × (co2_saturation - 350)
units.stop_mode: 'maturity' — simulation runs until TTc >= T_sum (crop reaches physiological maturity). No fixed day count.
units.max_days_safety: days — hard safety cap to prevent infinite runs. If maturity not reached by this day, sim stops and a warning is shown.
units.gdd_stages: °C·d cumulative TT thresholds for each growth stage (proportional to T_sum; verify with field records)

_DOCUMENTATION.fTemp_equation: fTemp = 0 if Tmean < T_base; (Tmean-T_base)/(T_opt-T_base) if T_base <= Tmean < T_opt; 1.0 if Tmean >= T_opt
_DOCUMENTATION.fHeat_equation: fHeat = 1 if Tmax <= T_heat_onset; 1-(Tmax-T_heat_onset)/(T_extreme-T_heat_onset) if T_heat_onset < Tmax < T_extreme; 0 if Tmax >= T_extreme
_DOCUMENTATION.fCO2_equation: fCO2 = 1 + s_co2*(CO2-350) for CO2<=700; = 1 + s_co2*350 for CO2>700 (saturates)
_DOCUMENTATION.fSolar_growth: fSolar = f_solar_max / (1 + exp(-0.01*(TTc - I50A))) when TTc <= (T_sum - I50B_dynamic)
_DOCUMENTATION.fSolar_senescence: fSolar = f_solar_max / (1 + exp(+0.01*(TTc - (T_sum - I50B_dynamic)))) when TTc > (T_sum - I50B_dynamic)
_DOCUMENTATION.I50B_update: I50B_dynamic += i_50maxH*(1-fHeat) + i_50maxW*(1-fWater) each day (stress accelerates senescence)
_DOCUMENTATION.biomass_equation: dB = RUE_eff * SRAD * fSolar * fTemp * min(fHeat, fWater), where RUE_eff = RUE * fCO2 * fertilizer_modifier

sweet_corn.name: Sweet Corn
sweet_corn._note_c4: Sweet corn (Zea mays saccharata) is a C4 crop. C4 photosynthesis is largely CO2-saturated at ambient levels, hence the very low S_CO2=0.01 (table value) vs 0.07 for C3 crops.
sweet_corn._note_stop: Simulation runs until TTc >= T_sum (1900 °C·d). Safety cap = 365 days to prevent infinite runs.

canopy._note: i_50b is the STARTING cultivar value. It increases daily via stress (see I50B_update equation above).

radiation._source_f_solar_max: estimated_zhao2019 — not in Table 1a; standard SIMPLE model value 0.95 used; verify if document specifies otherwise

stress._note_s_co2: Table 1a gives S_CO2=0.01 per 100ppm. Per-ppm value = 0.01/100 = 0.0001. C4 crop — minimal CO2 fertilization effect.
stress._note_s_water: Table 1a gives S_water=2.0. Used in: fWater = max(0, 1 - 2.0 * ARID). High sensitivity — crop reaches fWater=0 at ARID=0.5.

gdd_stages._note: Proportional to T_sum=1900. Verify against GSS0966 field records.

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato._note_c3: Tomato (Solanum lycopersicum) is a C3 crop. C3 photosynthesis responds significantly to elevated CO2 (S_CO2=0.07 vs 0.01 for C4).
tomato._note_stop: Simulation runs until TTc >= T_sum (2300 °C·d). Safety cap = 365 days to prevent infinite runs.

radiation._source_f_solar_max: estimated_zhao2019 — not in Table 1a; standard SIMPLE model value 0.95 used

stress._note_s_water: Table 1a gives S_water=2.5. High drought sensitivity — fWater=0 at ARID=0.4.

gdd_stages._note: Proportional to T_sum=2300. Tomato stages scaled from document page 2 example. Verify against Agriset761 field data.

carrot.name: Carrot
carrot._note_c3: Carrot (Daucus carota) is a C3 root crop. HI = 0.70 represents the large proportion of total biomass stored in the harvestable taproot.
carrot._note_stop: Simulation runs until TTc >= T_sum (2450 °C·d). Safety cap = 365 days to prevent infinite runs.

radiation._source_f_solar_max: estimated_zhao2019 — not in Table 1a; standard SIMPLE model value 0.95 used

stress._note_s_water: Table 1a gives S_water=2.0. fWater=0 at ARID=0.5.

gdd_stages._note: Proportional to T_sum=2450. Verify against Kazan F1 field records in Philippines highlands.
